close_reason: Fixed by preserving assistant tool-call ancestors when OM context pruning would otherwise return orphaned tool-result messages; added regression coverage and verified lint/test/typecheck.
description: Investigate and fix the pi-om-extension context pruning path so returned message windows never orphan a tool result from its originating assistant tool call. This appears to trigger OpenAI/Codex Responses errors like `No tool call found for function call output with call_id ...` after recent OM lifecycle changes. Add regression coverage for tool-call/tool-result adjacency and validate lint/test/typecheck for the package.

comments.text: Diagnosed the likely regression in `context` pruning: the OM extension could return a tool-result message without the matching assistant tool-call message when the published cursor starts at (or falls back to) a tool result boundary. Added `ensureToolCallPairing(...)` to expand pruned windows backward to include the required assistant tool-call ancestor, plus unit and extension regressions covering both normal pruning and latest-message fallback. Verified `npm run lint`, `npm run test`, and `npm run typecheck` at the repo root all pass cleanly.

title: Fix OM context pruning so tool-result windows keep matching tool calls
